title: minuartia procumbens var. extensa
intro: Minuartia procumbens var. extensa, also known as creeping whitlow-grass, is a plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Minuartia procumbens var. extensa can be prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for skin issues and digestive problems.
